task.title: Changing the DHCP client of NetworkManager

context.p: By default, NetworkManager uses its internal DHCP client. However, if you require a DHCP client with features that the built-in client does not provide, you can alternatively configure NetworkManager to use dhclient.
Note that RHEL does not provide dhcpcd and, therefore, NetworkManager cannot use this client.

step.cmd: Create the /etc/NetworkManager/conf.d/dhcp-client.conf file with the following content:

info.p: You can set the dhcp parameter to internal (default) or dhclient.

step.cmd: If you set the dhcp parameter to dhclient, install the dhcp-client package:

info.p: Note that the restart temporarily interrupts all network connections.

li.p: Search in the /var/log/messages log file for an entry similar to the following:
li.codeblock: Apr 26 09:54:19 server NetworkManager[27748]: <info> [1650959659.8483] dhcp-init: Using DHCP client 'dhclient'
li.p: This log entry confirms that NetworkManager uses dhclient as DHCP client.
